title: Opponent-Aware Undercut/Overcut Features
summary: Pit decisions are competitive, not purely individual.
rationale: Pit decisions are competitive, not purely individual. Public F1 strategy analysis and game-theoretic pit-stop work both emphasize that tyre wear only matters relative to nearby rivals, traffic, and whether an undercut or overcut is live.
implementation_hint: For each `(Year, Race, LapNumber)`, derive nearest-ahead/nearest-behind rivals by `Position`; compare tyre age, compound, lap delta, degradation, and stint against those neighbors; add flags like `within_undercut_window` when a driver is close enough in position and has fresher/slower-heat compound context. Also test local traffic density features: count of cars within +/-2 positions sharing older tyres or pit-vulnerable compounds.
expected_effect: Can capture racecraft effects missed by current same-lap z-scores, especially where pitting is triggered by a nearby rival rather than absolute tyre life.
risk: Without explicit time gaps, position-based proxies may be blunt and can overfit track-specific behavior.
